author: gameoffate2018
description: NavGO is an easy to use path finding tool meant for use when tile map based path finding is just not possible or is to complicated for the project at hand.
name: NavGO

releases.message: If Pathfinding completely fails, false is returned instead of an error message.
Introduces the "NAVGO.REMOVE_NODE(myUrl)" command. This can be used by messaging a navgo node hash("remove_node"). This will remove it from the tree without recalculating the entire path.
This release shows a custom built directional node and fixes a bug with the label so that it does not appear during runtime.
This release changes the look of the directional NavGo nodes so that during runtime and with debug enabled, they can show their ID number to the developer.
Minor bug fixes, removed unneeded collision from the NavGO nodes.
Version 12 introduces Non-sequential support for the directional nodes. Nodes can now be placed out of order and still handle navigation based off of the ID values.

## Directional Nodes
This release contains a new type of node called the 'NavGO_directionalNodeGO' or 'directional node' for short. This node can be used to create one way paths between nodes. This special type of node can be included or excluded from the rest of the nodes in the collection, meaning that linked paths can or cannot be generated through it. To use this new type of node, the functions NAVGO.IS_DIRECTIONS_READY and NAVGO.GET_PATH_FROM_DIRECTIONAL_ID have been added. 

To use the node in level, you must place it down, give it a number ID as well as an ID for the node it will point to.

This release features two new functions, NAVGO.GET_NODE_NEAREST_TO_POSITION and NAVGO.GET_NODES_IN_RANGE. 

Alternate messages will be sent from the NavGo Handler if no path can be completed.

NAVGO Functions for path finding (GENERATE_PATH and GENERATE_PATH_TO_RANDOM_NODE) will return an additional Boolean variable to show if paths have or have not been found. These functions will return an empty table if no path has been found.
Updated function name to be more clear that it is an internal function.
Added function: NAVGO.GET_NODE_NEAREST_TO_POSITION(position) this will fetch a node nearest to the position being requested.

Rework of back end code.
Pathfinding can now be done through the NavGo_Handler or the NavGo_Global singleton.
Some functions such as init, final, and redraw class can only be used through the handler.
Added a 'final' message to the NavGo handler so it can be cleared and used again.
Added 'return all nodes' command to the handler that will forward a list of node URLs to the sender.
This release adds the ability to have a random node selected from the tree. The URL of the node's base game object will be sent to the sender.

#Bug fix
Module address fixed to ensure working code.

#Release 1
This release holds the most basic elements of my NavGO. The full projects includes an example with a figure moving around a block using path finding.
